issue_title: Notebook starts running again after cell execution
issue_body:
I am not certain if this is a bug or some weird one-off issue that is happening on my end. 

Sometimes my notebook will start running again after executing a cell. It will not run other queued cells unless I manually interrupt the kernel, meaning that I have to wait for a cell to finish, interrupt the kernel, and then run the next cell.

This behavior begins sometime randomly after being restarted, never immediately after opening a notebook. Once the behavior starts, it continues until the notebook is restarted.

I have tried narrowing down the cause (is it a particular cell? library? size of data? garbage collection?), but have not been able to consistently reproduce the behavior. The same notebook can run for longer or shorter without the issue coming up, but it almost inevitably happens at some point.

This is VERY frustrating and I would appreciate any help at all, however big or small. Guidance on finding a way to reproduce this would also be wonderful, as I've tried the obvious things and am still stuck.

Thank you so much!

issue_title: don't print full environment in error messages when starting a new kernel
issue_body:
We just had someone post an error log from starting up a kernel that had their full environment variables listed. This often can contain sensitive security-related information (like passwords, private tokens, etc.), so we shouldn't list all of their environment by default (maybe just their path, which is often relevant?). The problem is that people will try to be helpful and post their logs, not realizing that they contain sensitive information.

I think to reproduce this, modify a kernelspec so that it won't start, or points to a nonexistent file, and then try starting a new notebook. Look at the server logs for the "Failed to run command".

My complex analysis is a bit rusty, and I'm getting confused by the handling of complex functions. Many functions are differentiable as complex functions (i.e. they are [holomorphic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holomorphic_function)) and their complex derivatives are implemented in autograd.

However there also seem to be functions, like `abs`, `var`, `angle` and others, which are not differentiable as complex functions, but they also have derivatives implemented. I'm assuming these derivatives treat the complex inputs as if they were 2D real valued vectors? This seems inconsistent to me.

Users can fairly easily replicate the second behaviour without these derivatives being defined, by manually decomposing their numbers into real and imaginary parts, so I would tentatively propose removing these pseudo-derivatives...

Apologies if I'm making some dumb mistake here...

issue_title: Critical: SDK still not compatible with V11
issue_body:
On September 31 the API services for V9/V10 will be shut down - only 3 weeks to left.
https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/bing_ads_api/2017/09/29/30-day-reminder-migrate-to-api-v11/

**But this SDK is still not compatible to V11!**

The problem are two hardcoded lines in the code where V9 is used instead of v11.


_bingads/reporting/reporting_operation.py_ on line 56:
`        self._service_client = ServiceClient('ReportingService', authorization_data, environment, 9, **suds_options)`

Change to:
`        self._service_client = ServiceClient('ReportingService', authorization_data, environment, 11, **suds_options)`


_bingads/reporting/reporting_service_manager.py_ on line 39:
`        self._service_client = ServiceClient('ReportingService', authorization_data, environment, 9, **suds_options)`

Change to:
`        self._service_client = ServiceClient('ReportingService', authorization_data, environment, 11, **suds_options)`

However this is just a dirty quickfix. The used version should not be hardcoded here.

issue_title: Review the API specification (replies and JSON schema)
issue_body:
I'm currently working on a small TypeScript client which represents the Core endpoints in a TypeScript environment. While doing this I noticed that
1. some JSON schema specifications don't match the provided examples (e.g. missing attributes)
2. some attributes are explicitly nullable (`anyOf: [ { "type": "string" }, { "type": "null" } ]`) while others are allowed to be absent (which would be the way to go I think)
3. some endpoints have no associated schema
4. most schemas have a weird format (are using single quotes and trailing commas), which isn't valid per JSON specification
5. some endpoints reply with error objects and some don't have errors, I'd like to propose to use HTTP errors there instead of error objects

in the [API specification](https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/blob/develop/docs/api-specs.md).

For creating the mentioned Core client I had to create own (spec-conform) JSON schema specifications, happy to contribute them and and adding additional ones.

I first tried to generate a client from the API spec, but in the current form it's unfortunately impossible. I'd like to suggest (and help ;)) to review them until the next breaking release when the deprecated ones get dropped.

---
(I enumerated these for separate discussion, if I should open multiple issues please leave me a message.)

In version 0.6.8:

AsyncConn.max_rdy_count is initialized to 2500 (line 175 in async.py) but the value from the actual nsqd server is never used. This means that Readers are effectively capped at 2500 max_in_flight.

It appears that the fix would be to parse max_rdy_count in AsyncConn._on_identify_response.

Reproduction steps:

1. `nsqd --max-rdy-count 10000 [...]`
2. Create a Python consumer: `nsq.Reader(..., max_in_flight=8000)`
3. Upon running the consumer, the following log message will appear: `WARNING:nsq.reader:[127.0.0.1:9999:POSTBACK] max RDY count 2500 < reader max in flight 8000, truncation possible`

Hi Peter,

Maybe I have some misunderstanding of your philosophy of pie, but I think for create/create_key_pair, it's not vey reasonable to only have "name" as the inputs.

 In real scenario, creating a key without any attributes probably is very rare. For example, for MAC, I need to set the crypto usage mask for the key in the create() operation. Of course I can add the attributes later but that's not very neat and also make the operation not atomic.

So do you think it's more reasonable to give them a list of attributes as the input instead of just "name". In that case, we can even get rid of "operation_policy".

I know this will make the user to build all the attributes. But I think that's necessary step to make the pie interface really useful in real scenario.

issue_title: bug: requests.request running endless in some cases
issue_body:
My router changes his ip every three days, and then this sdk does not reconnect to the server.
[This](https://github.com/matrix-org/matrix-python-sdk/blob/master/matrix_client/api.py#L558) request method does not return in that case, and so there is also no exception which could be handled.

Solution: add a timeout to the request.
I can do a pull request, but there are some points to discuss:
- The timeout has to be larger than the timeout defined in the query_params. (if available). Should we use 60 seconds by default (if no timeout is available), otherwise 2*query_params['timeout'] (`request_timeout = 2 * query_params.get('timeout', 60000)/1000`)
- Should we handle the possible timeout exception in listen_forever by default?

Moreover I have to search whether there are more requests in this code, and whether it is useful to add a timeout.

```

The validator callback needs to deal when `None` value is passed, for more information check [1]

[1] http://click.pocoo.org/6/options/#callbacks-and-eager-options

Currently Betelgeuse parses the test docstrings and assign fields in a way that does not apply to every scenario. This means that some of the default values may not be available on all Polarion projects, Polarion custom fields can be created per project basis and Betelgeuse will not be able to fill all of them, the user may want to apply its own rules to generate Requirements, field values, test case names so on and so forth.

The better way to solve this is allowing the user to specify a Python module as a customization module. On that module functions like `get_test_case_name` may be present and if they are they should be called in order to get the value. Continuing with the `get_test_case_name` example, that function can receive a testcase object along with the raw docstring, this way the user can create its own logic in order to create the test case name, for example the test case name can contain the value from some other field like the OS.

If no customization module is provided Betelgeuse will use its own rules and default values. The same will happen if the customization module is provided but the use didn't provide some specific function, then the default Betelgeuse behavior will be used. On the other hand, depending on the filed, Betelgeuse can expect so user configuration to be present, but currently I don't see any case of this.

issue_title: Missing coverage for mixin class when testing derived class?
issue_body:
I'm not sure if this is a bug or not.

Scenario:
I have an implementation of Levenberg-Marquard that I'm using to "invert" a function. Because I am interested in the broad category of all such functions (not just one particular form), I thought I'd be "clever" and implement this particular portion as a mixin that can be freely inherited by client classes. Unfortunately, the mixin itself is not directly testable (it requires that client classes provide certain key pieces of information, such as gradients and the forward operation which I am attempting to invert). 

When I look at test coverage reports, I see clear indication that I have run tests which exercise calls to the mixin's methods via its concrete subclasses / clients (I know because I currently have tests that fail after making these calls -- d'oh!). What is confusing to me is that when I look at the coverage reports, I see the entire mixin being marked as uncovered. 

What are reasonable expectations in this case? I can imagine an implementation that, were a test to fail, pytest / pytest-cov may drop any information related to that test (such as coverage) and that once those tests pass, I /should/ see the coverage on the base class? If not, what are the "right" ways to go about testing classes which are not directly instantiatable (e.g. mixins, traditional OO abstract base classes, ...) in a way that produces true estimates of test coverage for those classes? 

Thanks!

issue_title: Determine if *-auto should be a symlink
issue_body:
Unfortunately, the way that GitHub handles symlinks isn't ideal for `*-auto`. For example, here's a [symlink to a directory](https://github.com/certbot/testrepo2/blob/master/amul2). Symlinks to files are treated the same way. The raw contents of the symlink is simply the target's path, not the contents.

With this in mind I propose we leave `letsencrypt-auto-source` largely unmodified. Moving this directory or making it a symlink to `certbot-auto-source` will break `letsencrypt-auto`. I also think keeping both `letsencrypt-auto` and `certbot-auto` in the `letsencrypt-auto-source` directory is unnecessary as they will be the same file. This means both `certbot-auto` and `letsencrypt-auto` will be downloading `letsencrypt-auto-source/letsencrypt-auto` for updates, but this is an implementation detail and not a big deal in my opinion.

The other question we have to consider is about `*-auto`s in the root of our repo. `letsencrypt-auto` must remain in our repo in some form. All of our installation instructions use it and have done so since the beginning of public beta.

Do we want a `certbot-auto` in the root of our repo? I think we do as having `letsencrypt-auto` be the only `-auto` script in our repo when `certbot-auto` is the preferred form is a bit awkward. We could make one of the scripts a symlink to the other. The problem is this breaks anyone doing `wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/master/letsencrypt-auto` (or with `certbot-auto`). While I haven't seen anyone do this, we can avoid this potential problem by having full copies of both `certbot-auto` and `letsencrypt-auto` in the root directory. I think I prefer this approach over using symlinks.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Point users at fullchain.pem rather than cert.pem?
issue_body: Users who run standalone or manual get a message pointing them at a cert.pem file.  But actually, most users running modern webservers want fullchain.pem.

issue_title: Audit Apache plugin for guaranteed reversion
issue_body: We've had some apache tickets (eg #998 #1354 #1243) which imply that the plugin's revert-in-case-of-error mechanisms are imperfect.  Those completely block the release of the Apache plugin.  We need to perform an audit to ensure that the all of the Apache plugin's file writing operations, module enablement/disablement, and config editing are properly wrapped in proper reversion logic and try-except blocks that ensure reversion is attempted after any failure.

issue_title: Enable http-01 over TLS for the Manual Plugin (Was: Add Support for TLS-SNI-01 in Manual Plugin)
issue_body:
Looks like this is a [known TODO](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt/plugins/manual.py#L34), but I have a pressing need for it!

This is necessary for using Let's Encrypt with "serverless" (AWS Lambda) applications on Amazon's API Gateway (for instance with [Zappa](https://github.com/Miserlou/Zappa) or the [Serverless.org/JAWS Framework](https://github.com/serverless/serverless)), as there is no way to serve plaintext HTTP through the API Gateway.

As such, with Zappa we have a bootstrapping problem where we must first generate a self-signed cert for the initial domain deployment, then use the LE manual client in TLS-SNI-01 mode to generate our first LE certificate. Currently, this is impossible because this feature isn't implemented yet in the LE manual plugin.

I imagine the interface would be quite simple, simply add a "--challenge-mode" argument that defaults to http-01 but accepts tls-sni-01. The other change would be to the SimpleHTTPServer CMD_TEMPLATE to use ssl.wrap_socket on port 443.

I'd be happy to implement it if you'd accept that, but if somebody owns this part of the code already would like to step up and add this feature, that'd be much appreciated as well.

Thanks!

As part of my PhD research on code authorship, we calculated the Truck Factor (TF) of some popular GitHub repositories.

As you probably know, the [Truck (or Bus) Factor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bus_factor) designates the minimal number of developers that have to be hit by a truck (or quit) before a project is incapacitated. In our work, we consider that a system is in trouble if more than 50% of its files become orphan (i.e., without a main author).

More details on our work in this preprint: https://peerj.com/preprints/1233

We calculated the TF for **Powerline** and obtained a value of **1**.

The developer responsible for this TF is:

Nikolai Aleksandrovich (ZyX) - author of 97% of the files 

To validate our results, we would like to ask **Powerline** developers the following three brief questions:

(a) Do you agree that the listed developer is the main developer of **Powerline**?

(b) Do you agree that **Powerline** will be in trouble if the listed developer leave the project (e.g., if he wins in the lottery, to be less morbid)?

(c) Does **Powerline** have some characteristics that would attenuate the loss of the listed developer (e.g., detailed documentation)?

Thanks in advance for your collaboration,

Guilherme Avelino

UFMG, Brazil

issue_title: Documentation error:  utils.get_value attempts to __getitem__ before getattr
issue_body:
I had a problem with a very basic use case (see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49889233) and it turns out the explanation was that `Field.get_value()` tries `__getitem__` and only falls back to `getattr` in event of an error. 

The first section of the Quickstart reads: 

> Create a schema by defining a class with variables mapping attribute names to Field objects.

Strictly speaking, this is not true, since __getitem__ keys are not attribute names. This should be clarified somewhat exhaustively in the docs.

issue_title: Field 'only' parameter should only apply to serialization
Add option to fail on unexpected fields
issue_body:
Per the [docs](https://marshmallow.readthedocs.io/en/latest/extending.html#schemavalidation):

> Normally, unspecified field names are ignored by the validator. If you would like access to the original, raw input (e.g. to fail validation if an unknown field name is sent), add `pass_original=True` to your call to `validates_schema`.

If I'm trying to use marshmallow to do validation that the input is what I expect and I want to fail if I get unexpected data, is there really no way to have it fail without writing your own validator? (Apologies if I'm just missing something obvious in the docs - this seems very surprising as someone new to marshmallow).

If not, feature request: please could you add a `fail_on_unexpected` flag to accompany `strict` (which would have to default to `False` for backwards compatibility reasons? (I guess `strict` is some combination of hypothetical `fail_on_missing` and `validate` keywords?)

Different functions in `turicreate.text_analytics` use different default values for delimiters.

`count_words` `trim_rare_words` `tokenize` uses:
["\r", "\v", "\n", "\f", "\t", " "]

`count_ngrams` uses:
["\r", "\v", "\n", "\f", "\t", " ", "!", "#", "$", "%", "&", "'", "(", ")",
   "*", "+", ",", "-", ".", "/", ":", ";", "<", "=", ">", "?", "@", "[", "\\", "]",
   "^", "_", "`", "{", "|", "}", "~"]

I think we should always use the longer list.

In addition we should add the double quote character (ie`"`) to list, single quote character is already included.

This is a proposal to generalise the default schema we currently use internally.

Paging @slinnarsson, @gioelelm and @pl-ki for feedback. If everything is OK I can implement this, and Peter will be able to use it in his updates to the pipeline.

### Support for global attributes `..Name`, `..Key`, and `..SearchKey`

Currently, we use `Accession` as the "row key attribute", `Gene` as the default "row search key" attribute, and `CellID` as both "column key" attribute and "column search key" attribute. None of this is written down in the documentation.

It also ties the structure of our loom files to the current pipeline. Perhaps these labels will change in the future - they have already changed a few times internally in the past! 

Suggestion: add global attributes that state which of the row/column attributes are the key attributes. That way, writing scripts for the pipeline will be more "future proof", since it won't have to worry about changing labels. 

Similarly, we assume rows represents genes, and columns represents cells, but people might use loom for different purposes. I suggest adding a name attribute for that.

For backwards compatibility, we can use the above defaults as fall-backs if these attributes are missing.

The proposed schema ends up as:

- `rowName` (optional, is used to label the scatterplot view in `loom-viewer`. Defaults to "Genes" if `Gene` is present as a row attribute, "Rows" otherwise)
- `rowKey` (optional, _must_ contain a unique value for each row. Defaults to `Accession` if it is present in row attributes, uses row index numbers otherwise)
- `rowSearchKey` (optional, defaults to `Gene` if it is present as a row attributes, uses row index numbers otherwise)

- `colName` (optional, is used to label the scatterplot view in `loom-viewer`. Defaults to "Cells" if `CellID` is present in column attributes, "Columns" otherwise)
- `colKey` (optional, _must_ contain a unique value for each column. Defaults to `CellID` if it is present in column attributes, uses column index numbers otherwise)
- `colSearchkey` (optional, defaults to `CellID` if it is present in column attributes, uses column index numbers otherwise)

### Using `rowSearchKey` and `colSearchKey` for row/column getter functions

Currently, the documentation suggests the following way to access a row/column by Gene or CellID:

```python
>>> ds[np.logical_or(ds.Gene == "Actb", ds.Gene == "Gapdh"),:]
array([[  2.,   9.,   9., ...,   0.,  14.,   0.],
       [  0.,   1.,   4., ...,   0.,  14.,   3.]], dtype=float32)

>>> ds[:, ds.CellID == "AAACATACATTCTC-1"]
array([[ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       ...,
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.]], dtype=float32)
```

I think it would be convenient to create a helper function that would wrap this NumPy logic:

```python
>>> ds.getRows(["Actb", "Gapdh"])
array([[  2.,   9.,   9., ...,   0.,  14.,   0.],
       [  0.,   1.,   4., ...,   0.,  14.,   3.]], dtype=float32)

>>> ds.getColumns(["AAACATACATTCTC-1"])
array([[ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       ...,
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.]], dtype=float32)
```

These would use the `rowSearchKey` and `colSearchKey` attributes for choosing a default attribute, with the option for users to override this: `ds.getRows(list, searchKey=self.rowSearchKey)`

TODO list if this is all approved:

- [ ] add `rowName`, `rowKey`, `rowSearchKey`, `colName`, `colKey` and `colSearchKey` support to loom creation methods
  - [ ] `create`
  - [ ] `create_from_cellranger`
  - [ ] `_create_sparse`
- [ ] add `getRows` method to `LoomConnection`
- [ ] add `getColumns` method to `LoomConnection`

issue_title: Add a public constants module and include scipy as base requirement for pvlib
issue_body:
pvlib should have a public constants python module so that all the different models share the same constants and so that users can see what constants pvlib is using and match them if/when needed.

For example, the data values in a gold dataset for the single-diode model depend on the choice of constants, see [this PR](https://github.com/pvlib/pvlib-python/pull/426). Note that `scipy.constants` contains many useful physical constants traceable to [CODATA](http://www.codata.org/committees-and-groups/fundamental-physical-constants), as well as temperature conversion functions and the like.

We also have to make sure we distinguish between the same constant with different units, or agree to use only one version of each constant throughout the codebase.

I think we should wrap the `scipy.constants` and make `scipy` an import requirement for the "base" installation of pvlib. (This would also remove a lot of the test decorations and import checks around `scipy`, and `scipy.special.lambertw` is already needed for `pvsystem.v_from_i` and `pvsystem.i_from_v`.) Simple tests should be written to catch and audit when constants have changed.

I have a use case where I don't need/want an endpoint for every model, this seems to be in line w/ the spec which says "Complex data structures involving JSON objects and arrays are allowed as attribute values."

As an example w/ a model that has 1..N 'Source' objects.

```
class Source(models.Model):
    obj = models.ForeignKey(ParentObj, related_name='sources')
    url = models.URLField()
```

Would it be possible to have the serialization of 'ParentObj' to include ```'sources': [{'url': '...'}, {'url': '...}]```

(If not I'd be open to taking a swing at it & implementation suggestions would be welcome)

issue_title: Use mkLt to compare two bit vectors in Z3 Java
issue_body:
Hello,

I am trying to perform the following semantics in `Z3 Java` (note that the code is indeed written in `Scala` so the syntax looks different):

       val a = ctx.mkBV(12, 32).asInstance[BitVecExpr]
       val b = ctx.mkBV(5, 32).asInstance[BitVecExpr]

       ctx.mkLt(a, b)      
      // I want to compare a and b, and the comparison results are used for future reasoning.  Note that a/b could be bit vectors of complex symbolic formulas. 
   
It seems that function `mkLt` only accepts `ArithExpr` as parameters, so I type cast the above code into: 

        ctx.mkLt(a.asInstrance[ArithExpr], b.asInstance[ArithExpr])   

This code can be compiled, however, during runtime, I got an exception as:

    Exception in thread "main" java.lang.ClassCastException: com.microsoft.z3.BitVecExpr cannot be cast to com.microsoft.z3.ArithExpr

I suppose compare two bit vectors seems a reasonable operation, and I am not sure why it is not allowed. Am I missed anything here? Could anyone give me some help on this issue? Thank you!

issue_title: Missing interpretation for constant in model output by (get-model)
issue_body:
The code:

```
(declare-const xp Int)
(assert (> xp 10))
(assert  (forall ((x Int) (y Int))
               (=> (< x (- y xp))
                 (exists ((z Int))
	           (and (< x z)
	    	        (< z y))))))
(check-sat)
(get-model)

```
outputs:

```
sat
(model 
)

```
The model is empty, even if it should give an interpretation to xp.

By removing the first assertion, `(assert (> xp 10))`, the model is output as expected.

So now that we're on the topic of requirements (#15, #19) -- I'm not sure using `future` in a library like this (esp. `future.standard_library.install_aliases()`) is a good idea.

I mean, just importing `upcloud_api` as it is [will install magical `sys.meta_path` hooks](https://github.com/PythonCharmers/python-future/blob/master/src/future/standard_library/__init__.py#L521-L538).

Maybe just use `six` (as it's a dep too anyway)?

I just finished playing with fully-convolutional nets with Lasagne and ran into one small issue. Theano's `theano.tensor.nnet.softmax` op doesn't support 4D tensors. This is an issue when setting the nonlinearity of a Conv2DLayer to `lasagne.nonlinearities.softmax`.

It's possible to just replace the use of Theano's softmax with the equivalent code like this:
```python
e_x = theano.tensor.exp(x - x.max(axis=1, keepdims=True))
return e_x / e_x.sum(axis=1, keepdims=True)
```

and everything works fine. This might miss out on extra speed/stability optimizations done by the actual Theano softmax op though. I don't know if this issue is better raised here or with Theano.

Primarily for the sake of the web app, bring over some of the components:

* directory listing
* browse header
* breadcrumbs

We're in a bit of a midway transition here on some packages, namely that we've bumped `react`, `react-dom`, some related packages, and `jest`. We need to bring up `flow` and `immutable` at some point, they seem a bit tough (read: time consuming), so I'm tempted to just get these out.

The other thing I put us on the hook for is supporting the vdom transform and `object-to-react` for jupyterlab + classic notebook. Jupyterlab is easy because they use commonjs -- the only extra we should provide are typescript definitions. For notebook, I'll figure out how to produce AMD and publish to bower.

Is there anything else you can think of (@lgeiger, @BenRussert?) that you'd want to see finished for when we release the next wave of packages that Hydrogen will end up with? I'm tempted to make many of these major bumps because of the change of `React` underneath to the next major version -- we can probably cause breakage otherwise if not everything gets upgraded together.

I see what looks like a deadlock when running the test suite with paramiko installed, on linux + 2.7:

```
py.test --collect-only odo/
...
platform linux2 -- Python 2.7.6 -- py-1.4.26 -- pytest-2.6.4
collecting 175 items / 12 skipped
```

I suspect a deadlock because the issue disappears iff I remove the `ssh.connect` in https://github.com/ContinuumIO/odo/blob/master/odo/backends/tests/test_ssh.py#L24, and strace shows a litany of:

```
[pid 10354] futex(0x180ef0a0, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, NULL <unfinished ...>
[pid 10381] futex(0x180ef0a0, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1 <unfinished ...>
[pid 10354] <... futex resumed> )       = -1 EAGAIN (Resource temporarily unavailable)
[pid 10381] <... futex resumed> )       = 0
[pid 10354] select(0, NULL, NULL, NULL, {0, 8000} <unfinished ...>
[pid 10381] select(0, NULL, NULL, NULL, {0, 100000} <unfinished ...>
[pid 10354] <... select resumed> )      = 0 (Timeout)
[pid 10354] select(0, NULL, NULL, NULL, {0, 16000}) = 0 (Timeout)
[pid 10354] select(0, NULL, NULL, NULL, {0, 32000}) = 0 (Timeout)
[pid 10354] select(0, NULL, NULL, NULL, {0, 34890}
```

You may not want to do network IO in imports, as this may cause some thread creation (https://docs.python.org/2/library/threading.html#importing-in-threaded-code). I would suggest to instead implement the skip functionality using memoization.

issue_title: [radio] mIsCcaEnabled flag behaviour is not well defined
issue_body:
Expected behaviour for this flag is not well defined.

Current mac (and link raw) behaviour for frames with `mIsCcaEnabled` set to `false` looks like this:
- Start CSMA-CA random backoff
- Send frame without CCA

But most common case for frames with CCA disabled is that we just transmit frame without backoff as well.

We should define common CSMA-CA random backoff behaviour when this flag is set to `false` (as now the description says only about CCA) or introduce additional flag for CSMA-CA.

I have faced this problem when using link raw API as there is no option to send frame without CSMA-CA backoff with `OPENTHREAD_CONFIG_ENABLE_SOFTWARE_RETRANSMIT = 1` and I was expecting that setting this flag to false will do the job.

issue_title: REED and FED receiving of multicasts from neighboring routers
issue_body:
According to section 4.7.7.4 of thread spec 1.1.1 REEDs and FEDs must maintain one-way links with neighboring routers so that the REED/FED can receive MAC-secured multicasts from them. Currently in openthread FEDs/REEDs use link requests & accepts as required, but drop incoming MAC-secured transmissions that are not from their parents.

This is because the method of validating a MAC source address uses GetNeighbor(), which switches on the current role:
https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/1f6528e6c9a600b7f04128ed8f22e841107361f6/src/core/mac/mac.cpp#L1572-L1576
Of course, this behaviour of GetNeighbor is usually needed, so perhaps a special case in Mac.cpp to check the router table too, in the case of an FFD child receiving a broadcast frame?

Example Logs (I used an address query from a non-parent router to generate):
Child establishing one way link:
```
[6] [INFO]-MLE-----: Receive Advertisement (fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f,0x8000)
[6] [INFO]-MLE-----: Send Link Request (fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f)
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: Sent IPv6 UDP msg, len:92, chksum:1cb5, to:2e1859bb93779a0f, sec:no, prio:high
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: src: fe80:0:0:0:1432:68b9:eb2d:53b7
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: dst: fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: Received IPv6 UDP msg, len:97, chksum:8e81, from:2e1859bb93779a0f, sec:no, prio:low, rss:-20.0
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: src: fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f
[6] [INFO]-MAC-----: dst: fe80:0:0:0:1432:68b9:eb2d:53b7
[6] [INFO]-MLE-----: Receive Link Accept (fe80:0:0:0:2c18:59bb:9377:9a0f,0x8000)
```

Router table of child:
```
router table[6] [INFO]---------: execute command: router table

| ID | RLOC16 | Next Hop | Path Cost | LQ In | LQ Out | Age | Extended MAC     |

issue_title: Losing prints when the device will be shutdown
issue_body:
<!--
Please feel free to delete any sections that aren't relevant.
-->

<!-- mark with x between the [ ] -->
I have:
- [x] searched open and closed issues for duplicates

### Bug description

### Steps to reproduce
- Create a new python file, paste and adapt the lines below:
```
from escpos import printer as escpos_printer
from escpos.escpos import EscposIO
import time

with EscposIO(escpos_printer.Network(192.168.1.1, port=9100), autocut=True) as printer_socket:
    printer_socket.writelines('Test Line 1')
    time.sleep(5) #Here i'm simulating a long-running print -> Turn-off the printer
    printer_socket.writelines('Test Line 2')
    printer_socket.writelines('Test Line 3')
    printer_socket.writelines('Test Line 4')  -> Turn-on the printer
    printer_socket.writelines('Test Line 5')
```
- Try to print launching the script
- When the execution will hit the sleep line, turn-off the printer and keep it turned-off (simulating a power failure)...no exception at all.
- Turn on the printer (on the line that prints: "Test Line 4") , as you can see, the printer will not print anything, losing, in fact, everything.

### The question is:
How i can do a sort of retry if no exception will be raised? maybe i have to check for every line that i'm printing if the printer it's online? (but it's not a good solution, in my opinion...)

Thanks,

Simone.

I am trying to get UnitTests to work:

`AWS_ASK_ROLE=true python -m unittest discover . "test*.py"`

I get **4 failures** and they all seem to be for the same underlying reason.

Am I right that this clever coalesce function [# Ask Role (Option priority = ARGS, ENV_VAR, DEFAULT)](https://github.com/cevoaustralia/aws-google-auth/blob/91cb4f764655082631b61d8c1ba07742dbe9f6f0/aws_google_auth/__init__.py#L92)
```
        config.ask_role = bool(coalesce(
        args.ask_role,
        os.getenv('AWS_ASK_ROLE'),
        config.ask_role))
```

Will always return 'False' as the value in `args` is not `None` it is set to `False` by  Default in `configuration.py`
_In [Class Configuration](https://github.com/cevoaustralia/aws-google-auth/blob/91cb4f764655082631b61d8c1ba07742dbe9f6f0/aws_google_auth/configuration.py#L21)_

```
        # Set up some defaults. These can be overridden as fit.
        self.ask_role = False
        ...
        ...
```
In this case, the value can never be overridden by environment??

(This also maybe signals the importance of Unit Tests in Travis Build ....)
Happy to be shot down!

issue_title: Switch to pyldap with 'bytes_mode=False' likely to break many Python2 consumers
issue_body:
The changes in https://github.com/django-ldapdb/django-ldapdb/commit/46f474438b9529192c62eefb38e6a211964ae696 mean that all Python2 consumers must ensure that any and all string data passed to the library are unicode strings (`u'...'` or `unicode('...')`). Failure to do this conversion in the application prior to calling ldapdb will result in errors like: `All provided fields *must* be text when bytes mode is off; got 'cn'`.

This breaking change is probably worth documenting in the Changelog and README. Having read through https://github.com/pyldap/pyldap/issues/1 I think I understand why this change is being made, but users should be warned/educated about the need to ensure that `unicode` is used.

issue_title: GooglePlusAuth Causes Errors When Used with an `id_token`
issue_body:
As far as I can tell, it's because the `id_token` is passed to [`do_auth`](https://github.com/python-social-auth/social-core/blob/40d700650c14711bda5637e2279612e630ef52cc/social_core/backends/google.py#L137-L144), which causes it to be passed to [`user_data`](https://github.com/python-social-auth/social-core/blob/40d700650c14711bda5637e2279612e630ef52cc/social_core/backends/google.py#L53-L61) in turn (via [`do_auth`](https://github.com/python-social-auth/social-core/blob/ef0a4d4aeedc04ec7d068fbbb317a72e6e182a4b/social_core/backends/oauth.py#L403)).

But an `id_token` apparently can't be used as an `access_token` and causes a...

> Daily Limit for Unauthenticated Use Exceeded. Continued use requires signup.

...error. Which I'm guessing is because the request is unauthenticated.

The tokeninfo endpoint for google+ should contain everything the backend needs, though. If there's a way to check for client side flow in `user_data` (like passing in a `token_type` argument or something?), it's possible that the user_data method can skip making an additional request.

Was able to work around this by just subclasses `GooglePlusAuth` and returning `None` from `user_data` since I'm only using the client-side flow.

```
from social_core.backends.google import GooglePlusAuth

class CustomGooglePlusAuth(GooglePlusAuth):
    def user_data(self, *args, **kwargs):
        return None
```

It is not production ready yet, but it seems to have some traction: https://www.w3.org/Webauthn/

A python implementation is at
https://github.com/duo-labs/py_webauthn

issue_title: Undefined stack exports should serialize as "undefined"
issue_body:
In https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/issues/1223, I used an undefined property as a stack output:

```
let siteBucket = new aws.s3.Bucket("s3-website-bucket");
exports.bucketName = siteBucket.bucketName; // there is no property 'bucketName' on s3.Bucket!
```

In this case, the stack output is simply ignored: running this program shows no stack exports. Instead, Pulumi should use the string "undefined", so that customers can find their error more easily.

For others, from the "installer" script:

```
VERSION=$(curl --fail --silent -L "https://pulumi.io/latest-version");
TARBALL_URL="https://get.pulumi.com/releases/sdk/pulumi-v${VERSION}-${OS}-x64.tar.gz"
```

So, for me, the current latest release is here: https://get.pulumi.com/releases/sdk/pulumi-v0.14.0-linux-x64.tar.gz

It'd be great if these were:
1. linked from the README
2. linked from the site
3. were included in the GitHub tagged releases

Maybe they are and I missed it?

I think these are going to be far more appetizing to Pulumi's intended audience than an opaque `curl|sh` "installer".

issue_title: Implement possibility to edit the status of an activity
Map inside a HBox not visible by default
issue_body:
I've noticed a surprising behavior when adding several maps to an ipywidget HBox. Consider this code:

```python
import ipywidgets
from ipyleaflet import Map

center, zoom = [43.6, 1.44], 5
widgets.HBox([Map(center=center, zoom=zoom), Map(center=center, zoom=zoom)])
```
This doesn't show anything visible. The map elements actually do get inserted into the DOM but they have a width of zero.

One possible workaround is to set a layout on the map:

```python
import ipywidgets
from ipyleaflet import Map

center, zoom = [43.6, 1.44], 5
layout = widgets.Layout(width='50%')
widgets.HBox([
    Map(center=center, zoom=zoom, layout=layout),
    Map(center=center, zoom=zoom, layout=layout)
])
```
Now the maps are visible. Should there be a minimum width on maps?

Demo patch for that upcoming.

issue_title: Split Exception types: processing schema and runtime
issue_body:
Right now we have one python file where error granularity is defined.

We should make explicit a separation:

* Exceptions raised while processing the schema (such as when a window dtc refers to a non-existing datagroup in a streaming schema)
* Exceptions raised while executing the transformation

The reason is that both errors are handled differently:

The 1st type of error is the user's fault. Something's wrong with the input, and there should be an explicit, properly formatted, tested and reviewed error message to be displayed.

The 2nd is an internal error. If there are no errors of the 1st type, 2nd errors shouldn't be produced. A full stack trace should be reported in this case.
